title: xport lock-step manifest

area.description: Optional label for this manifest file. Used as a grouping key in harness output. Defaults to 'root' for the top-level file and to the filename stem for included files.

includes.description: Relative paths to sub-manifests. Top-level `upstreams` and `sites` maps override any same-keyed entries in included manifests.

upstreams.description: Named upstream submodules. Referenced by rows[].upstream on file-fork, version-pin, feature-parity, spec-conformance rows. Omit when the manifest only has lang-parity rows.

submodule.description: Submodule path, relative to repo root.

sites.description: Named sibling ports (typically per-language). Referenced by rows[].ports.<site> on lang-parity rows. Omit when the manifest has no lang-parity rows.

path.description: Path to the port's root directory, relative to repo root.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the manifest. Starts with lowercase letter or digit; remaining characters are letters/digits/hyphens. Kebab-case preferred, but camelCase segments are allowed (e.g. `export-findNodeAt` when the id mirrors an API name).

criticality.description: Stay-in-step importance (1 = cosmetic, 10 = security-sensitive). Harness surfaces high-criticality drift louder.

conformance_test.description: Path to a test that enforces behavior parity (modulo documented deviations). Strongly recommended — static checks can't catch silent behavioral drift.

notes.description: Free-form context — why this row exists, what gotchas to watch for.

local.description: Path to our ported file, relative to repo root.

upstream_path.description: Path to the source file within the upstream submodule.

forked_at_sha.description: Full 40-char SHA of the upstream commit we forked from. Harness runs `git log <sha>..HEAD -- <upstream_path>` to surface drift.

deviations.description: Human-readable list of intentional differences. Zero deviations = use upstream directly; don't fork.

oneOf.description: A local file derived from an upstream file with intentional modifications. Drift = upstream moved forward without us.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the manifest. Starts with lowercase letter or digit; remaining characters are letters/digits/hyphens. Kebab-case preferred, but camelCase segments are allowed (e.g. `export-findNodeAt` when the id mirrors an API name).

criticality.description: Stay-in-step importance (1 = cosmetic, 10 = security-sensitive). Harness surfaces high-criticality drift louder.

conformance_test.description: Path to a test that enforces behavior parity (modulo documented deviations). Strongly recommended — static checks can't catch silent behavioral drift.

notes.description: Free-form context — why this row exists, what gotchas to watch for.

pinned_sha.description: Full 40-char SHA the submodule is pinned to.

pinned_tag.description: Human-readable release tag (e.g., `v3.2.1`). Optional — the SHA is authoritative.

upgrade_policy.description: track-latest: any new release is actionable; major-gate: only major bumps require review; locked: explicit decision per upgrade.

oneOf.description: A submodule pinned to an upstream release. Drift = upstream cut a new release we haven't adopted.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the manifest. Starts with lowercase letter or digit; remaining characters are letters/digits/hyphens. Kebab-case preferred, but camelCase segments are allowed (e.g. `export-findNodeAt` when the id mirrors an API name).

criticality.description: Stay-in-step importance (1 = cosmetic, 10 = security-sensitive). Harness surfaces high-criticality drift louder.

conformance_test.description: Path to a test that enforces behavior parity (modulo documented deviations). Strongly recommended — static checks can't catch silent behavioral drift.

notes.description: Free-form context — why this row exists, what gotchas to watch for.

local_area.description: Path to the local module/directory implementing the feature. Code pattern scan targets this directory (excluding test files).

test_area.description: Optional path to the directory where tests for this feature live. When absent, the harness searches inside `local_area`.

code_patterns.description: Regex patterns the local implementation must contain. Prefer anchored patterns (function signatures) over loose keywords to avoid comment false positives.

test_patterns.description: Regex patterns the test suite must contain.

fixture_check.description: Golden-input verification. Prefer snapshot-based diffs over hardcoded counts (brittleness lesson from sdxgen's lock-step-features).

oneOf.description: A behavioral feature reimplemented locally to match upstream behavior. Three-pillar validation: code patterns, test patterns, fixture snapshots.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the manifest. Starts with lowercase letter or digit; remaining characters are letters/digits/hyphens. Kebab-case preferred, but camelCase segments are allowed (e.g. `export-findNodeAt` when the id mirrors an API name).

criticality.description: Stay-in-step importance (1 = cosmetic, 10 = security-sensitive). Harness surfaces high-criticality drift louder.

conformance_test.description: Path to a test that enforces behavior parity (modulo documented deviations). Strongly recommended — static checks can't catch silent behavioral drift.

notes.description: Free-form context — why this row exists, what gotchas to watch for.

spec_path.description: Path within the upstream submodule to the spec document, if applicable.

oneOf.description: A local reimplementation of an external specification. Drift = the spec was revised.

id.description: Stable identifier, unique within the manifest. Starts with lowercase letter or digit; remaining characters are letters/digits/hyphens. Kebab-case preferred, but camelCase segments are allowed (e.g. `export-findNodeAt` when the id mirrors an API name).

category.description: Grouping tag. `rejected` is reserved for anti-patterns (every port must be opt-out; reintroduction exits 2).

criticality.description: Stay-in-step importance (1 = cosmetic, 10 = security-sensitive). Harness surfaces high-criticality drift louder.

conformance_test.description: Path to a test that enforces behavior parity (modulo documented deviations). Strongly recommended — static checks can't catch silent behavioral drift.

notes.description: Free-form context — why this row exists, what gotchas to watch for.

assertions.description: Open-ended assertion list. Each has a `kind` string the harness dispatches on. Unknown kinds are skipped with a log line.

matrix_files.description: For inventory rows that index other xport-lang-*.json files. Paths relative to this manifest.

reason.description: Required when status is `opt-out`. Explain why.

path.description: Optional path to the port's implementation of this row. Useful for module-inventory rows where each language points at a different directory.

note.description: Optional free-form note attached to a specific port's status.

additionalProperties.description: Per-port status for a lang-parity row. `implemented` = port meets assertions; `opt-out` = port consciously skips, requires non-empty `reason`.

ports.description: Per-site status. Keys must match top-level `sites`.

oneOf.description: N sibling language ports of one spec within a single project. Drift = one port diverged from its siblings.

description: Unified lock-step manifest shared across Socket repos. One schema, all cases — `kind` discriminator on each row selects which flavor of lock-step applies.
